For context, this well-known Qari has never once condemned the U.S-Israeli aggression on Iran that has claimed civilian lives, including…" 🇰🇼 Quran.com has removed Mishary Rashid Alafasy as its default Quran reciter after he posted controversial comments on X supporting Trump's attacks on Iran . He had long been the default reciter, with his voice automatically playing for millions of users. Following widespread backlash from Muslims outraged by his pro-Trump, anti-Iran remarks amid ongoing U.S.-Israeli aggression, the popular website quietly demoted him from the default position in early April 2026. His recitations are still available for manual selection, but no longer play automatically. Quran.com has not issued any official statement.

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 After reviewing flight data of a Northrop Grumman MQ-4C Triton, it was found the drone is operating over the Persian Gulf near Iran’s coastline. — This drone took off from the Sigonella Air Base in Sicily. Currently, this drone is flying in a figure-eight pattern near the city of Bushehr, Iran, where the country's only nuclear power plant is located nearby. — This drone is moving at an altitude of 14,000 meters and at an approximate speed of 400 kilometers per hour.

Iran says it doesn’t expect a total blackout even if the U.S. attacks its power plants. An official told Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting that “highly diversified energy production sources,” along with the grid’s “geographic dispersion” and “interconnectedness,” would allow the country to keep supplying at least partial power.

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Iran’s FM spokesperson, Esmail Baghaei: : The US 15-point plan is absolutely unacceptable / We conveyed our positions through intermediaries — When the US 15-point plan was proposed, we stated that such proposals are both extremely greedy and unreasonable and illogical, and absolutely unacceptable to us. — Based on our interests and considerations, we have documented the set of demands we had and have; we are not ashamed to loudly express our legitimate and logical demands and requests. — That the Islamic Republic of Iran quickly and courageously presents its views in response to a plan should by no means be interpreted as yielding to the enemy. — We have prepared our responses and will explicitly inform when it is necessary to announce them; it is not strange that intermediaries convey the viewpoints of both sides. These positions were initially raised and conveyed through intermediaries, and naturally, this message transfer continues.

🇮🇷 🇵🇰 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Reuters is reporting that Pakistan's army chief, Field Marshal Asim Munir, has been in contact "all night long" with U.S. Vice President JD Vance, special envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi for a settlement deal. 🔸 Under the proposal, a ceasefire would take effect immediately, reopening the Strait of Hormuz, with 15–20 days to finalise a broader settlement. The deal, tentatively dubbed the "Islamabad Accord," would include a regional framework for the strait, with final in-person talks in Islamabad. 🔸 There was no immediate response from U.S. and ‌Iranian officials. ⁠Pakistan's foreign office spokesperson Tahir Andrabi declined comment. Iranian officials have previously told Reuters that Tehran was seeking a permanent ceasefire with guarantees they will not be attacked again by the U.S. and Israel. 🔸 Iran has received messages from mediators including Pakistan, Turkey and Egypt according to the outlet. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

A third Turkish-owned tanker, Ocean Thunder, cleared the Strait of Hormuz overnight. It was carrying Iraqi crude to Malaysia. Türkiye now has 3 ships safely out of the Gulf area since the conflict began in late Feb 2026. 12 ships remain stuck.

Trump warned he could destroy Iran’s power plants if it doesn’t reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday. However, mediators say he still wants a cease-fire and may compromise. Iran remains skeptical, suspecting the U.S. could use any pause to prepare further attacks. Source: WSJ

This is one of the primary maritime export hubs, where Russian occupiers ship up to 20% of their total oil volumes. tweet

Nuno Felix This is a damn solid account of the operation to extract the downed US WSO out of Iran. Details about the rescue op for the U.S. Weapon Systems Officer, via a U.S. military official: "The mountain top area on the left is where the WSO was hiding (he ejected 5ish miles northwest of there). The right area is the makeshift landing strip where they landed 2 C-130s and had 4 MH-6 Little Birds. "One Little Bird flew to that mountain top area and rescued the WSO and brought him back to the landing strip. And of course the two C-130s' nose gears got stuck in the dirt. So after a few hours they had to bring in three AFSOC Dash-8s to fly out the rescued WSO and the 100 or so personnel involved in the op." 1/2 - Michael Weiss tweet

France’s central bank sold 129 tonnes of French gold stored in New York, bought equivalent gold in Europe that meets current standards, and recorded a €12.8bn gain due to higher gold prices. All French gold reserves are now held in Paris.

🇱🇧 ✝️ In March 2026, a 26-meter-tall statue of Christ the Redeemer was erected on Jabal Al-Salib (Mount of the Cross) in Al Qaa, Lebanon. Designed by architect George Makhlouf and spearheaded by local resident Fady Awad, the fiberglass and steel monument stands as a symbol of protection and hope for a region currently facing deep conflict. The location is highly symbolic: Al Qaa is a predominantly Christian town near the Syrian border that was once a frontline in the fight against ISIS. By placing one of the Middle East's largest Christian monuments on this former battlefield, organizers aim to honor the site's spiritual history and encourage future religious tourism. The structure includes a 16-meter statue atop a 10-meter base, with plans to open a church at the site shortly. Let's only hope that the Epstein syndicate won't bomb it to rubble . 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

↩️ Reply to Clash Report: "Iranian official to Reuters: Tehran has received Pakistan's proposal and is reviewing it; it will not accept deadlines or any pressure. Tehran will not reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for a temporary ceasefire." Pakistan’s proposed two-phase deal 1) Immediate Phase • Ceasefire starts right away. • Strait of Hormuz reopens immediately (key for global oil). 2) Follow-up Phase (15–20 days) • Final comprehensive peace deal negotiated. • Tentatively called the “Islamabad Accord”. The final deal may include: • Iran agrees to limits on nuclear program (no weapons). In return: • Sanctions relief. • Release of frozen assets. • Broader regional framework for Hormuz security. Source: Reuters
